mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tight labor market conditions that historically precede wage-driven consumption growth, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and real GDP growth indicates synchronized expansion in construction activity and aggregate demand, which historically amplifies corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 when both HOUST and GDPC1 show positive 3-month momentum, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects capacity utilization normalization and supply chain restoration, which directly improves earnings guidance and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains negative when INDPRO rises > 1.0% month-over-month during identified recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.3 percent triggers market expectations of accelerated monetary tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL monthly growth exceeds 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail and food services sales reflects underlying consumer spending resilience, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of the broader macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant difference between high and low RSAFS growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable federal funds rate indicates healthy employment growth without premature monetary restraint, which historically sustains equity risk appetite and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS move in the specified joint direction, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.8 million units combined with elevated consumer prices signal late-cycle overheating that typically precedes aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which historically compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to predict drawdowns during identified late-cycle periods.
